steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject “Mary Madden of Ohio” is singular—one person. The prepositional phrase “of Ohio” modifies the subject but doesn't change its singularity.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and tense
steps.content:
A singular subject requires a singular verb, and the past time reference requires past tense. B) “was” is the only form that is both singular and past tense. The other choices all use plural verbs, violating subject-verb agreement.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
